answers.question: How would you like to proceed?
Any feedback or changes needed?
answers.custom_answer:
[Line 24]: If you mean that we need to group these things into a seven-part construction, and that construction is an object we want to repeat, that is considered a phrase in our terminology. It can consist of:
- an English word or phrase
- three repetitions in Spanish
- two longer phrases that use the phrase and are in Spanish
All of this is considered one parrot repetition.

> Audio is a segment set, not a single URL. Replace audio_url: String with audioSegmentPaths: [String] (mirroring ParrotPhrase.segmentPaths) so a card reuses the exact El Loro audio engine already shipped. A "play" = one full pass through the loop, not one file.
[Line 30]: we used this 7-parts construction (1-english + 3*spanish one word + 2* full phrases) only for the first "memory" and it's fine.. the first time it's actually good to memorize as long as it is needed, still considering that this is the very first phase even though it is being repeated many times in circle. Once the phrase was played X loops - it goes to the next phase, but there it is being played less times (say round X*0.7 but not least than once) and so on until at the later stages it's gonna be one repetition only. So together with the dates when task repetition should be scheduled, we need to mark how many times repetition will happen.

> Define where Memory Cards live. Recommendation: SwiftData is the operational store (matches ParrotPhrase), with a memory_cards Supabase table mirrore
[Line 36]: We should not overburden our supabase database. If we store all the WAV, MP3, or whatever audio in the device memory, our servers will blow up.
What we should mirror to the server is the queue and the playback statistics:
- When it was scheduled
- Whether it was actually played or not
- Similar status information
We need to send events (probably to a webhook or similar) so we can keep track and monitor everything at the database level. The raw audio and the logic for triggering should stay on the device.

> The spec defines max_near_term_load = 40 and a near_term_load metric but no gate uses it. Either wire it into a gate (recommended — it is the real backpressure metric) or remo
[Line 39]: Well yeah probably we didn't explicitly mention that as a backend thing to remember but yes you are right. We need to capture this of course as a setting. And yes we need to enable the user to change it in its user settings or somewhere.

> The five visible life stages
[Line 71]: We need to reflect the stages here, and they must match our backend stages. The progress positioning should be something like:
Loro: now Loro knows the word, and it will be forgotten after...
 - - One hour
- Three days
- One month
- One year
 - Five years

> his 5-act compression is the only phase language the user ev
[Line 77]: Yes, we can play with things like not letting Loro forget things. These words can be forgotten if you don’t practice, and he’ll forget them after blah blah blah blah blah blah. We can show a table of forecasted forgettingness. That would be cool.

> Animation inventory
[Line 90]: No, it’s not needed now. We will not use these eggs and things. We will use backward time goals.

Our goal is to increase long-term memory and prevent Loro from forgetting things. Maybe we can show a timeline where each stage is represented.. I don't know about the animation, but it should be a simple timeline or some representation that clearly shows the short term versus the longer term. We cannot compare a one-hour view to a five-year view with a graph. Let's simplify that for now.

> 5-act token art (egg → known feather
[Line 98]: Yes, we need to visualize it, but I don’t know how to visualize this journey based on the memory lens. Maybe Loro will have a bigger head, somehow, like five years is a very, very big mature parrot. We cannot change the parrot’s skin. The parrot’s skin will stay the same because it is the same parrot. It just changes the words it adds to the memory, and those words have their own memory timeline, not the bird’s.

Maybe we use font. I know something is written in the sand is one hour, something is more stable is one week. Maybe we can use the materials as the measure of time and stability. Less than one hour, yeah, you are right, it should be egg and feather. Let’s make it act, and indeed we will name it time.
